SELECT date_string, parseDateTimeBestEffortOrZero(date_string) AS parsed_date FROM test_dates;
CREATE TABLE test_logs (log_id Int32, log_message String, log_timestamp String);
INSERT INTO test_logs VALUES (1, 'Log entry 1', '2023-10-01 12:00:00'), (2, 'Log entry 2', 'not a date'), (3, 'Log entry 3', '2023/10/01 12:00:00');
SELECT log_id, log_message, parseDateTimeBestEffortOrZero(log_timestamp) AS parsed_timestamp FROM test_logs WHERE parsed_timestamp IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE test_join (id Int32, date_string String);
SELECT t1.id, t1.date_string, parseDateTimeBestEffortOrZero(t1.date_string) AS parsed_date FROM test_join AS t1 JOIN (SELECT 1 AS id) AS t2 ON t1.id = t2.id;
SELECT t1.date_string, parseDateTimeBestEffortOrZero(t1.date_string) AS parsed_date FROM test_join AS t1 WHERE parsed_date >= '2023-10-01';
WITH (SELECT date_string FROM test_dates) AS subquery_dates SELECT parseDateTimeBestEffortOrZero(date_string) AS best_effort_date FROM subquery_dates;
CREATE TABLE complex_dates (id Int32, date1 String, date2 String);
INSERT INTO complex_dates VALUES (1, '2023-10-01', '2023-10-31'), (2, 'invalid', '2023-10-15'), (3, NULL, 'invalid date');
SELECT id, parseDateTimeBestEffortOrZero(date1) AS parsed_date1, parseDateTimeBestEffortOrZero(date2) AS parsed_date2 FROM complex_dates;
CREATE TABLE date_range (start_date String, end_date String);
SELECT * FROM date_range WHERE parseDateTimeBestEffortOrZero(start_date) <= parseDateTimeBestEffortOrZero(end_date);
